description_long: This is a well-worn leather baseball glove, featuring a tan-brown exterior with visible signs of use and weathering. The glove has a deep pocket and a cross-laced webbing between the thumb and index finger, providing flexibility and support for catching baseballs. Black leather laces are prominently threaded along the perimeter, webbing, and finger stalls, adding both structure and visual contrast. The palm area is smooth and slightly discolored from repeated use, while the back of the glove displays the word 'STRIKE' faintly written across the fingers. The glove has five distinct finger stalls, each padded and separated, with reinforced stitching for durability. No visible brand logo or wrist strap adjustment mechanism is present, and the wrist opening is simple and open.
description: A tan, weathered leather baseball glove with black lacing, cross-laced webbing, and five padded finger stalls. The glove shows signs of heavy use and has 'STRIKE' written on the back.
description_view_0: The glove is shown from an angled front, highlighting the deep pocket, cross-laced webbing, and black lacing along the thumb and perimeter.
description_view_1: The back of the glove displays the word 'STRIKE' written across the finger stalls, with black lacing visible at the top and sides.
description_view_2: The glove is viewed from the side, clearly showing the cross-laced webbing and the open wrist area, as well as the structure of the thumb and pinky sections.
description_view_3: The palm side of the glove is visible, showing the smooth, worn leather surface, finger stall openings, and the deep catching pocket.
attribute_finger_stalls: Five finger stalls, each individually padded and separated, with reinforced stitching; standard arrangement for a baseball glove.
attribute_lacing_style: Cross-laced pattern on the webbing, single-laced along the perimeter and finger stalls, with prominent black laces throughout.
attribute_wrist_strap_design: Open wrist design with no visible closure mechanism or adjustment features.
